cards.back: An array that stores elements in a single row or dimension

cards.front: What is the purpose of the keyword 'const' when declaring an array?
cards.back: To ensure that the array cannot be modified after initialization

cards.front: What is the difference between array indexing and array slicing?
cards.back: Array indexing refers to accessing a specific element by its index, while array slicing refers to accessing a subset of elements by specifying a range of indices

cards.front: What is the purpose of the 'new' keyword when declaring an array in JavaScript?
cards.back: To dynamically allocate memory and create a new array object

cards.front: What is the difference between a 1D array and a 2D array?
cards.back: A 1D array stores elements in a single row or dimension, while a 2D array stores elements in multiple rows and columns
